steps.title: Identify the y-intercept and slope sign
steps.content: The line of best fit passes through the origin (approximately (0, 0)), so the y-intercept is 0. The line slopes upward from left to right, indicating a positive slope. This immediately eliminates choice B (negative slope) and choices C and D (horizontal lines with nonzero y-intercepts).

steps.title: Calculate the slope using two points on the line
steps.content:
Reading from the line of best fit, it passes through approximately (1, 1.1) and (5, 5.5). Using the slope formula:
$$m = \frac{\Delta y}{\Delta x} = \frac{5.5 - 1.1}{5 - 1} = \frac{4.4}{4} = 1.1$$

steps.title: Write the equation and match to the answer
steps.content: With slope $m = 1.1$ and y-intercept $b = 0$, the equation of the line is $y = 1.1x$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
